title: Green Seattle: East Duwamish Greenbelt | United Way of King County
content: The East Duwamish Greenbelt is an urban forest that offers green space, plant and wildlife habitat, walking trails, and a chance to reconnect with nature.

title: East Duwamish Greenbelt - Parks | seattle.gov
content: Located along the I-5 corridor on Beacon Hill, the East Duwamish Greenbelt is a series of parcels that form a natural area.

title: For those asking about the Duwamish greenbelt trails - my walk ...

title: Welcome to the Jungle…I mean the East Duwamish Greenbelt | The Beacon Hill Beat
content: Signs of people still living in the greenbelt. :   Walking down the trail from the Holgate side. With a name like the Jungle and a detrimental public image it is no wonder people are prone to stay away from the area. But recently, the jungle has undergone major changes in order to help get crime out and the community in. Most recently, with the implementation of the Mountains-to-Sound Trail and a effort to promote the area by its proper name, the East Duwamish Greenbelt. Coming down to the trail on the Holgate side, it is difficult to get to as a pedestrian. One has to walk down a narrow rocky path along the roadside and has to cross the busy road in order to reach the path. As a crime prevention technique the trail hopefully will help, but as a visitor to the area, it still feels like the East Duwamish Greenbelt is in a transition.
